preflight_quotes.1_bug_doc_smoking_gun: The 'smoking gun' is the absence of any path that calls `emitObservation` for fauna. Plants survived the broken overlap-callback wiring because the `addInteractable`-based interact path is independent. Animals have no equivalent fallback.
preflight_quotes.2_index_js_attachInteractionZone_body: Lines 206-233: `const player = scene.player?.sprite ?? scene.player ?? null; if (player && scene.physics?.add?.overlap) { try { scene.physics.add.overlap(player, zone, () => { if (!entity.isObservable()) return; _emitObservation(scene, entity, 'proximity'); }); } catch { /* swallow — physics not ready */ } }`
preflight_quotes.3_substrate_spec_lifecycle: From §9: 'Interaction zones — re-attached on scene mount.' This confirms re-attaching the overlap at a deferred time (post-create) is in-spec and expected behavior. From §10: 'Zero [per-frame cost]. The substrate has no update() hook. Interaction zones are event-driven' — using scene.events.once('update') is a one-time cost at mount, consistent with the zero-per-frame model.
preflight_quotes.4_LocalSceneBase_player_construction: LocalSceneBase.js line 155: `this.createWorld();` — subclass registerEntity runs here. Line 163: `this.player = new Player(this, px, py);` — player constructed AFTER createWorld. No `postcreate` or custom event is emitted anywhere in the create() method (lines 141-241); create() ends at line 241 with no custom event emission.

notes.lifecycle_event_chosen: update — scene.events.once('update', _wireOverlap). Verified against LocalSceneBase.js lines 141-241: the create() body does NOT emit any 'postcreate', 'postCreate', 'ready', or 'start' event after constructing this.player at line 163. The first reliable post-create hook available is the Phaser built-in 'update' event which fires on the first game-loop tick after create() returns, at which point this.player is guaranteed to be assigned (line 163 runs before create() returns at line 241).
notes.hmr_fast_path_proof: Lines 255-258 of modified index.js: `const playerNow = scene.player?.sprite ?? scene.player ?? null; if (playerNow) { // Fast path: player already exists (HMR / scene-restart case). _wireOverlap(); }` — when scene.player is truthy at registration time, _wireOverlap() is called synchronously, identical to the previous behavior.
notes.double_wire_guard_proof: Lines 241-242 of modified index.js: `function _wireOverlap() { if (zone._ecologyOverlapWired) return; ... zone._ecologyOverlapWired = true; }` — the flag is set on the zone object after the first successful wiring and checked at entry to abort re-entrant or duplicate calls.
notes.shutdown_leak_concern_addressed: scene.events.once() self-detaches after firing (Phaser's built-in one-shot behavior). If the scene is destroyed before the first 'update' tick fires, the listener reference lives only on the scene's own EventEmitter instance — it dies with the scene's event emitter when the scene is destroyed. Phaser's Scene.events emitter is destroyed on scene destroy/shutdown. No manual cleanup is required. This was verified to be Phaser's actual behavior: scene.events is a Phaser.Events.EventEmitter owned by the Scene plugin; it is removed on Scene.shutdown. The existing _ensureShutdownHook (lines 276-295) already handles zone/entity cleanup; the deferred listener adds no additional leak surface.
notes.no_scene_files_modified: Confirmed. `git diff --stat` shows only src/renderer/game/systems/ecology/index.js changed. No file under src/renderer/game/scenes/ was modified.
notes.no_data_files_modified: Confirmed. No file under src/renderer/game/data/ was modified.
notes.other_ecology_files_unchanged: Confirmed. Only index.js was modified. EcologyEntity.js, ecologyState.js, ecologyTicker.js, ecologyEvents.js, speciesResolver.js, ecologyAssetManifest.js, ecologyQueries.js are byte-identical to their pre-dispatch state.
runtime_caveats: Verdict tag remains design-only-implementation. The overlap wiring path has never been exercised at runtime — this fix resolves the structural timing bug but runtime validation (walk near javelina, confirm state.observations) is required to upgrade to runtime-validated.

Coyote is night-only per data/ecology.js TIME_BEHAVIOR.night.activeAnimals — even with the overlap wired correctly, coyote will not emit observations during day-time sessions because entity.active=false at day-time. This is correct behavior, not a regression.

The deferred 'update' listener fires once per zone on the first game tick. If scene physics is somehow not yet ready on the first tick (edge case in test harnesses), _wireOverlap silently no-ops (the try/catch + null-guard remain intact). In production Phaser, physics is always ready by the first update tick.

next_agent_suggestions: user-runtime-validation (walk near javelina in StreetBlockScene; confirm 'javelina' in state.observations; food_chain_tracker advances 4/8 → 5/8)
